SEARCH
Package org.unlaxer.tramli

Interface FlowStore

All Known Implementing Classes:
InMemoryFlowStore
public interface FlowStore
Persistence contract for flow instances.

Threading

FlowEngine assumes single-threaded access per flow instance. Implementations MUST ensure that concurrent calls to loadForUpdate(java.lang.String, org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowDefinition<S>) for the same flowId are serialized (e.g., SELECT FOR UPDATE, or application-level locking).

Atomicity

create(org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowInstance<?>)/save(org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowInstance<?>) and recordTransition(java.lang.String, org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowState, org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowState, java.lang.String, org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowContext) calls between them form a logical unit. Implementations SHOULD execute them within a single transaction where possible. If partial writes occur, the flow state (save(org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowInstance<?>)) is authoritative over the transition log.

Optimistic Locking

FlowInstance.version() supports optimistic locking. Implementations SHOULD increment version on save(org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowInstance<?>) and reject saves with stale versions.

Serialization

FlowInstance contains a FlowDefinition reference which holds lambdas and cannot be serialized. Persist only the instance metadata and use FlowInstance.restore(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowDefinition<S>, org.unlaxer.tramli.FlowContext, S, java.time.Instant, java.time.Instant, int, int, java.lang.String) to reconstruct at load time, re-attaching the current FlowDefinition. For FlowContext attributes, serialize using Class.getName() as key and your chosen format for values.
